top_signals.title: France Sticks to Plans to Narrow Deficit as Iran War Weighs
top_signals.summary: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure reiterated the government’s goal to reduce the deficit to 5% of output this year and below 3% in 2029 even as the Iran conflict weighs on the economy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Sri Lanka stuns with 100-bp rate hike as Iran war rattles currency, fuels inflation - Reuters
top_signals.summary: Sri Lanka stuns with 100-bp rate hike as Iran war rattles currency, fuels inflation  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Transforming the Electrical Grid to Power AI
top_signals.summary: AI’s rapid growth is pushing global energy demand skyward, forcing electrical grids to expand and modernize. Now, companies from China to Nigeria are investing in new tech to power the future. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: How did Jay Powell do at the Fed?
top_signals.summary: The outgoing chair has made some mistakes, but his decision to stand up to Trump was heroic
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Dutch authorities arrest men suspected of providing infrastructure for Russian cyber operations
top_signals.summary: Investigators seized more than 800 servers as they arrested two men suspected of violating European sanctions and assisting pro-Russian cyberattacks and disinformation campaigns.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Synthetic Biology, Drones, and AI: The Risks of Dual-Use Technologies
top_signals.summary:
Is it too late to stop criminals and American adversaries from exploiting AI to conduct cyberattacks or design novel pathogens? Has regulation kept pace with the threat civilian drones pose to critical infrastructure? AI researcher Lennart Heim, Army drone strategist Paul Lushenko, and CEO of Sentinel Bio Claire Qureshi join Jonathan to discuss the trade-offs between protecting the public and letting the private sector forge ahead. The conversation gets into synthetic DNA, the risk of drones at the FIFA World Cup, and whether the U.S. government should get early access to Silicon Valley’s newest large language models.Image: T. T. Paris

The post Synthetic Biology, Drones, and AI: The Risks of Dual-Use Technologies appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Lufthansa Says Fuel Supply Stable, Urges Summer Bookings
top_signals.summary: Deutsche Lufthansa AG reassured customers that fuel supply is stable and urged passengers to “absolutely” book summer vacations, an appeal that comes as Europe’s largest airline group depends on peak travel demand to meet its financial goals.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: HK Banks Tighten Scrutiny of Chinese Clients After Trading Curbs
top_signals.summary: Hong Kong banks are ramping up scrutiny of mainland Chinese clients opening savings and investment accounts, part of a broader push to stem capital flight after Beijing launched an unprecedented crackdown on illegal cross-border trading.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Microsoft: Domain Controller lookup may fail on Windows Server 2016
top_signals.summary: Microsoft has confirmed a new known issue affecting Windows Server 2016 systems that causes domain controller lookups to fail after installing the KB5087537 May 2026 security update. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: CISA to allow researchers to report vulnerabilities to exploited bugs catalog
top_signals.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced the creation of a nomination form on Thursday that they said enables “researchers, vendors, and industry partners” to report bugs that need to be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
